next_steps: Install Quotivity integration in HubSpot sandbox
Demo scheduled for May 13th at 2:00 PM Central to show new bundle features
Send onboarding overview doc with implementation prerequisites
Decision on CPQ tool path by end of May

jtbd.job: Replace aging custom quoting tool before ERP go-live without disrupting sales operations
jtbd.context: Company is mid-ERP implementation (NetSuite, targeting August completion). The legacy quoting tool was built in-house by their COO years ago and they have no intention of carrying it forward. CPQ must be selected by end of May and rolled out before ERP goes live.
jtbd.desired_outcome: CPQ tool selected, configured, tested, and adopted by sales team before August ERP rollout

jtbd.job: Configure and quote multi-product bundles with individually adjustable sub-items
jtbd.context: Their core quoting need involves configurable bundles (especially ducting bundles) where products can be added/removed and quantities adjusted at the sub-item level. They describe this as 'bundle in name, but BOM in reality.' Less than 10 items per bundle typically.
jtbd.desired_outcome: Sales reps can build configurable bundles with dynamic sub-items, adjust individual quantities, and produce accurate quotes quickly

jtbd.job: Consolidate quoting into a tool that integrates with HubSpot CRM and complements NetSuite ERP
jtbd.context: They have product data in NetSuite (PIM), customer data in HubSpot, and need a quoting tool that bridges both. They initially tried to solve quoting and e-commerce in one platform (BigCommerce) but it failed. Now looking for the right layer-on tool for HubSpot.
jtbd.desired_outcome: Single quoting tool that lives in HubSpot, syncs with NetSuite product data, and handles their configure-to-order complexity

pains.pain: Legacy custom quoting tool is aging and must be retired before ERP transition
pains.trigger: ERP implementation (NetSuite) targeting August completion creates a hard deadline. The custom tool cannot be carried forward, and the CPQ replacement must be rolled out and adopted before the ERP goes live.
pains.cost: Entire quoting workflow at risk if not replaced in time. ERP rollout timeline could slip. Sales team faces disruption if forced to adopt both a new ERP and new quoting tool simultaneously.
pains.urgency_reason: Decision needed by end of May 2026. Implementation must complete before August ERP go-live.

pains.pain: No CPQ tool evaluated can match their custom tool's configurable bundle capabilities
pains.trigger: After evaluating BigCommerce, NetSuite CPQ, and HubSpot's bundling beta, none could handle configurable bundles with individual sub-item quantity management and add/remove flexibility. The prospect described feeling like 'a snowflake.'
pains.cost: Months of evaluation time spent on BigCommerce (most of the time since last conversation). Delayed CPQ decision compresses implementation timeline. Leadership frustrated by lack of options.
pains.urgency_reason: Every week of delayed decision shortens the implementation window before August deadline

pains.pain: HubSpot's native bundling beta is stalled and cannot meet their timeline
pains.trigger: They got into a private beta for HubSpot's bundling tool, but the tool lost its developer, is behind schedule, and full functionality is not expected until Q3. The prospect needs to move faster than that.
pains.cost: Waiting for HubSpot means missing the August ERP deadline. The bundle line item display (parent with indented sub-items) they need is not functional in the beta.
pains.urgency_reason: HubSpot's Q3 timeline is too late for their August ERP go-live

pains.pain: NetSuite CPQ was too complex for configuration and setup
pains.trigger: Evaluated NetSuite's native CPQ during the tool selection process. Complexity of configuration, setup, and brand switching was a turnoff for leadership.
pains.cost: Evaluation time spent. Leadership lost confidence in native ERP quoting as a viable path.

pains.pain: BigCommerce CPQ-like functionality could not handle configurable bundles
pains.trigger: Spent significant time trying to combine e-commerce and quoting needs into one platform. BigCommerce could not manage individual sub-item quantities or add/remove items from bundles.
pains.cost: Most of the evaluation period (roughly two months) spent pursuing a dead end. Delayed the overall CPQ decision.

pains.pain: Tight implementation window creates risk of sales team disruption
pains.trigger: With decision needed by end of May and rollout required before August, the six-week implementation timeline means starting by mid-May at the latest.
pains.cost: If implementation slips, sales team faces simultaneous CPQ and ERP rollout. Training and adoption quality degrades under time pressure.
pains.urgency_reason: Six-week implementation window starting mid-May to hit August deadline

workflow_reality.current_process: Sales team uses a custom-built quoting tool developed by their COO. The tool handles configurable bundles (especially ducting bundles) where sub-items can be added/removed and quantities adjusted individually. Bundles are typically less than 10 items. The tool was considered elite for its time but has aged. Company is transitioning to NetSuite ERP and HubSpot CRM, and the custom tool will not carry forward.
tools_in_use: NetSuite ERP (about one-third through implementation, targeting August completion)
Custom quoting tool (legacy, built in-house, slated for retirement)
Custom e-commerce platform (also home-brew, also needs replacement)
brittleness_indicators: Custom tool built by COO who is not a dedicated developer, creating maintenance and knowledge concentration risk
Both the quoting tool and e-commerce platform are home-brew and need replacement simultaneously
ERP transition in progress adds complexity to any quoting tool change
No off-the-shelf tool has replicated the custom tool's configurable bundle capabilities, creating a uniqueness trap

evaluated.name: BigCommerce
evaluated.what_they_liked: Could potentially consolidate e-commerce and quoting into one product database. CPQ-like functionality was promising.
evaluated.why_they_passed: Could not manage individual sub-item quantities within bundles. Could not add/remove items from bundles dynamically. Not a full CPQ.
evaluated.key_quote: The ability to manage the quantities of the items in your bundle individually on those sub-items, and the ability to add and remove those items to modify the bundle, just were not there.

evaluated.name: NetSuite CPQ
evaluated.what_they_liked: Native to their incoming ERP. Would not require additional integration.
evaluated.why_they_passed: Too much complexity in configuration and setup. Bouncing between brands was a turnoff for leadership.
evaluated.key_quote: There's just too much complexity to their tool from a configuration and setup standpoint.

evaluated.name: HubSpot CPQ (Bundling Beta)
evaluated.what_they_liked: Product catalog with bundles gets close to what they need. The planned display of parent items with indented sub-items is what they want.
evaluated.why_they_passed: Tool lost its developer and is behind schedule. Full functionality not expected until Q3. The line item display for bundles does not work yet. Cannot wait.
evaluated.key_quote: Their ability to get there just isn't there at the moment, and the clock's ticking for us.

current_workaround.description: Custom-built quoting tool developed internally by their COO. Handles configurable bundles with dynamic sub-item management. Being retired as part of ERP transition.
current_workaround.adequacy: Functionally capable but aging. Was elite for its time. Cannot be carried forward into the new tech stack (HubSpot + NetSuite).

quotes.quote: We feel a little bit like a snowflake because our custom tool has some capabilities that nobody else has been able to show us.
quotes.usage_notes: Captures the manufacturing buyer's frustration with CPQ tools that cannot handle configure-to-order bundles. Strong for content targeting manufacturers who feel their quoting needs are too unique for off-the-shelf tools.

quotes.quote: It's kind of bundle in name, but really BOM in reality.
quotes.usage_notes: Precise language that distinguishes static product bundles from configurable bills of material. Captures the exact gap most CPQ tools fail to address for manufacturers. Strong keyword signal for configure-to-order content.

quotes.quote: The ability to manage the quantities of the items in your bundle individually on those sub-items, and the ability to add and remove those items to modify the bundle, just were not there.
quotes.usage_notes: Specific articulation of what configurable bundles require: individual sub-item quantity control and dynamic add/remove. Directly maps to Quotivity's bundle configurator roadmap. Strong for feature-benefit content.

quotes.quote: Their ability to get there just isn't there at the moment, and the clock's ticking for us.
quotes.usage_notes: Captures the urgency gap between HubSpot's development timeline and the buyer's implementation deadline. Strong for competitive positioning content about HubSpot CPQ limitations.

quotes.quote: There's just too much complexity to their tool from a configuration and setup standpoint.
quotes.usage_notes: Validates the ERP quoting weakness: powerful but too complex to configure. Useful for content positioning Quotivity against NetSuite CPQ and other ERP-based quoting.

quotes.quote: We've got a tight window here, and that's why it's becoming urgent.
quotes.usage_notes: Illustrates how ERP implementations create forcing functions for CPQ decisions. Useful for content about implementation timing and the ERP-to-CPQ sequencing problem.

quotes.quote: We really got fixated on the idea that in one product database I can handle my e-commerce store and my quoting tool and do the configuration, and we tried really hard to make that work.
quotes.usage_notes: Shows the allure of consolidation and why buyers pursue it even when it fails. Useful for content about the right architecture for manufacturing quoting: ERP for fulfillment, CRM for sales, CPQ for quoting.

quotes.quote: We do not have any intention of carrying forward our custom tool.
quotes.usage_notes: Clean statement of intent to move off legacy systems. Useful for content targeting companies replacing home-built quoting tools.

value_language: constrain what your sales reps can do
guide your sales reps through the process
roll it out to the sales team

deviant_terms.term: bundle in name, but BOM in reality
deviant_terms.meaning: Their bundles are not fixed product groupings but configurable bills of material where components can be added, removed, and individually adjusted. This distinction is core to their quoting complexity.
deviant_terms.frequency: used once but clearly the central concept of their requirement

deviant_terms.meaning: Self-description for feeling like their quoting needs are uniquely complex and unserved by available tools. Reveals both frustration and a degree of pride in their operational sophistication.
Their category for tools that augment an existing platform (HubSpot, NetSuite) rather than replacing it. They evaluated tools in three buckets: layer-on, native, and third-party.
deviant_terms.frequency: used once but represents their mental model for categorizing solutions

deviant_terms.meaning: Shorthand for the ERP-driven deadline pressure. The CPQ decision is coupled to the NetSuite go-live and cannot slip.
